Posted on April 29, Full Time Litigation Northeast Office

If you need reasonable accommodation in order to complete the application, interview, or some other portion of the selection process for a job at Earthjustice, please contact us at jobs@earthjustice.org.

At this time, new applications are no longer being accepted for this position.

  • If you have already applied but have not received an update yet, you remain under consideration for this role.
  • All applicants will hear from our team, whether to schedule an interview or to let you know we are not moving forward with your candidacy for this particular opportunity. Thank you for your patience.

For additional career opportunities at Earthjustice, please see all of our open job positions. We have many offices and hiring teams nationwide, and Earthjustice is growing.

We appreciate your interest in Earthjustice.

raw_description: We’re always looking for passionate, talented engineers and data scientists who are looking for mission driven work to protect our democracy. We may not have an opening now, but if that sounds like you, we want to meet you! Apply below to join our talent pool. On a semi-regular basis, we’ll reach out to people who have applied via our talent pool to get to know you and your skillset. We typically open roles for full-time or contractors based on project needs, which we can’t predict ahead of time. Generally, we have a breadth of long-term and short-term projects each with a variety of product, engineering, and data science needs. If you’re interested in working to Protect Democracy, we encourage you to apply, so we can get to know you now! That way when the need arises, we can reach back out and get to work fast. We look forward to hearing from you! title: Portfolio Analyst
pay_candidate_snippets: This is an exciting and challenging opportunity to work in OEGEN where we our ambition is to onboard and manage £billions of assets over the next 5 years.
raw_description: Help us make a big green dent in the universe. We're on a mission to drive the global renewable energy revolution, and we need many more people to help us make our vision a reality. Octopus Energy Generation (OEGEN) is a leader in sourcing, investing in, and managing a wide range of utility-scale renewable energy assets. As we continue to expand our investment base, we are dedicated to building a diverse team that reflects the communities we serve. Currently, we manage a global portfolio of over £7 billion in renewable energy assets. We are committed to delivering best-in-class responsible investment activity, offering like-minded investors the means and products to invest their capital sustainably. It is our ambition to change the entire energy lifecycle and make every green electron matter, delivering the best outcomes for customers, investors, the environment and society, accelerating the transition to net zero. About the role: We are seeking a Portfolio Analyst to join the OEGEN Funds Team, reporting to a Senior Portfolio Manager. For your portfolio of assets, your role is to understand and deliver against all investor requirements which includes reporting, valuing portfolio investments, forecasting and calculating distributions, debt management and investor communications. In this role, you will deliver accurate and high-quality outputs whilst supporting the drive to continuously improve our fund management processes. You will support onboarding new investments and capit
description: For your portfolio of assets, your role is to understand and deliver against all investor which includes reporting, valuing portfolio investments, forecasting and calculating distributions, debt management and investor communications. In this role, you will deliver accurate and high-quality outputs whilst supporting the drive to continuously improve our fund management processes. You will support onboarding new investments and capital recycling programmes as well as refinancing activities. This is an exciting and challenging opportunity to work in OEGEN where we our ambition is to onboard and manage £billions of assets over the 5 years. raw_description: Who We Are:. Being naked is the #1 most sustainable option. We're #2. Since, we've been on a mission to bring sustainable fashion to everyone. Named one of Fast Company’s Brands That Matter and winning a Best Carbon Footprint award, we have big goals like being Climate Positive by, Circular by, and pushing the whole industry forward along the way. Our work has gotten love in Drapers and Sourcing Journal, and TIME wrote about how great our CEO, Hali Borenstein, is. Basically, we're saving the Earth and looking damn good doing it. We're a global brand with 50+ stores (and counting) around the world, and our own sustainable factory in LA. We innovate across categories like accessories, swimwear, and sleepwear, and we reach millions of people with campaigns like this, this and this. None of this work is possible without the incredible people behind it. We're a mission-based company that invests in an inclusive culture, so we can innovate together and ensure everyone has the space to grow, thrive, and belong. And starting right now, we want to do all that with you. Senior Knitwear Designer:. Reformation has an exciting opportunity for a Senior Knitwear Designer to be a key member of our in-house Design team, leading the knitwear business. This individual will lead design for and support the growth of one of our largest and highest-potential categories, driving the creative vision from concept, to yarn and stitch development through final execution. You will be respo
description: This individual will lead design for and support the growth of one of our largest and highest-potential categories, driving the creative vision from concept, to yarn and stitch development through final execution. You will be responsible for translating directional global trends into wearable, customer-right knit pieces that feel distinctly Reformation. The ideal candidate is both highly creative and deeply technical, with mastery of knit construction and a strong commercial instinct. Own design, fit and execution for the knitwear business driving both creative and commercial results, along with key Merchandising and Buying category partners. Drive results by managing cross-functional efforts and maintaining strong relationships with domestic and overseas technical design, product development, and production partners.

Named one of Fast Company’s Brands That Matter and winning a Best Carbon Footprint award, we have big goals like being Climate Positive by, Circular by, and pushing the whole industry forward along the way. We innovate across categories like accessories, swimwear, and sleepwear, and we reach millions of people with campaigns like this, this and this. None of this work is possible without the incredible people behind it. We're a mission-based company that invests in an inclusive culture, so we can innovate together and ensure everyone has the space to grow, thrive, and belong. And starting right now, we want to do all that with you. The Role: Sr. Logistics Manager. Onsite 5 days a week out of Vernon HQ. We are looking for a Sr. Logistics Manager who will be responsible for the day to day management of carrier relations, product distribution, and shipping networks for a growing omni-channel direct-to-consumer business (ecommerce, wholesale, and retail). You will work closely with our internal teams to manage and develop a first-class
description: Logistics Manager who will be responsible for the day to day management of carrier relations, product distribution, and shipping networks for a growing omni-channel direct-to-consumer business (ecommerce, wholesale, and retail). You will work closely with our internal teams to manage and develop a first-class logistics operation that supports the company's growth and fast turning inventory, both domestically and internationally. Lead annual parcel RFP and ongoing rate negotiations with FedEx, UPS, USPS, regional carriers, and international partners. Lead and support the resolution of in transit package-related issues and act as an information resource for all things related to day to day shipping operations. Provide visibility and transparency to key partners across the business on shipping service levels.

raw_description: Who we are:. Since, we've been on a mission to bring sustainable fashion to everyone. Named one of Fast Company’s Brands That Matter and winning a Best Carbon Footprint award, we have big goals like being Climate Positive by, Circular by, and pushing the whole industry forward along the way. Our work has gotten love in Drapers and Sourcing Journal, and TIME wrote about how great our CEO, Hali Borenstein, is. Basically, we're saving the Earth and looking damn good doing it. We're a global brand with 50+ stores (and counting) around the world, and our own sustainable factory in LA. We innovate across categories like accessories, swimwear, and sleepwear, and we reach millions of people with campaigns like this, this and this. None of this work is possible without the incredible people behind it. We're a mission-based company that invests in an inclusive culture, so we can innovate together and ensure everyone has the space to grow, thrive, and belong. And starting right now, we want to do all that with you. Work Location/ Schedule:. Available to work a minimum of 20 hours per week,. Available to work a minimum of 3 days per week, weekends included. Able to work flexible schedule (days, nights, weekends, & holidays if required to meet the needs of the business). title: Director of Corporate Marketing
raw_description: Our recruitment team will come from an official calstart. CALSTART is a mission-driven industry organization focused on transportation decarbonization and clean air for all. We are seeking a strategic, hands-on Director of Corporate Marketing who can shape and tell our story with impact, translating complex concepts into clear value propositions for diverse audiences. The ideal candidate will have strong messaging and product marketing experience, experience leading agencies and internal teams, and a solid understanding of the electric vehicle space and fleet applications. In this role, you will own CALSTART's external brand marketing narrative, ensuring consistent, differentiated messaging across all channels, while leading development of value propositions, audience personas, and positioning that resonate with executives, fleets, policymakers, vendors, and partners

title: Digital Engagement Specialist
raw_salary: Title:     Digital Engagement SpecialistSupervisor:   Director of MarketingLocation: Flexible/remote; preference for Washington, DC areaSalary:  Estimated at $62,280 – $69,200*; Based on experience Application Deadline: May 30th, 2026Position Summary:This is a full-time position with American Bird Conservancy (ABC). American Bird Conservancy is a fast-growing, results-focused nonprofit seeking employees with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to make a difference for birds and their habitats throughout the Americas. Inspired by the wonder of birds, ABC achieves lasting results for the bird species most in need while also benefiting human communities, biodiversity, and the planet’s fragile climate.The Digital Engagement Specialist helps advance ABC’s mission by expanding and deepening relationships with priority audiences through creative use of digital products and channels. Leading the production and analysis of virtual events, the Digital Engagement Specialist creates compelling content that inspires, engages, grows, converts, and steers audiences in an omnichannel communications and marketing ecosystem. The position will play a key role in expanding the reach and awareness of ABC, while also motivating people to take key actions that benefit birds and advance ABC's mission, including fundraising, list growth, and advocacy. The ideal candidate has experience and proven success managing and/or hosting virtual and/or live events, using digital marketing platforms to produce emails, graphics, videos, and other forms of multimedia content, managing multiple forms of constituent engagement, generating content that meets key goals, and working collaboratively both internally and externally with colleagues, partners, influencers, organizations, and brands.Primary Duties:Virtual Events1. Execute a comprehensive virtual event strategy that raises awareness of the ABC brand, engages followers, cultivates donors, and drives relevant actions to grow the ABC email list and generate revenue. This includes:a. Generating ideas and achieving internal alignment on regular live events that achieve cross-departmental goals and outcomesb. Coordinating logistics, including scheduling, production, and documentation, for multiple live events at any given timec. Managing and assisting event speakers, including relationship-building, scheduling, preparation, and follow-upd. Managing external event support, including live translators, including the hiring and contracting process, scheduling, and all associated preparation and follow-upe. Developing and producing digital assets to support and market events, including videos, emails, graphics, and presentationsf. Providing technical and constituent engagement support before, during, and after each eventg. Conducting long-range planning, scheduling, and relationship-buildingh. Defining, tracking, reporting on, and analyzing metrics, both quantitative and qualitative i. Devising strategies to consistently improve outcomes 2. Work with ABC’s Multimedia Producer to produce recordings. This includes:a. Capturing and editing recordings b. Creating short-form content from recordings, including both video clips and sound bites 3. Contribute to the generation of revenue, both restricted and unrestricted, through helping to advance an effective omnichannel marketing and fundraising strategy via live events.Social Media and Constituent Engagement4. Work in conjunction with ABC’s Social Media Specialist to to grow and expand ABC’s social media platforms (potentially including Reddit, Tumblr, Discord, Twitch) and to support constituent engagement on organic and paid media across all ABC social media platforms, including but not limited to managing direct messages, comments, tags, and relevant discussions that include reference or connection to ABC.5. Manage ABC's mobile messaging inbox.6. Maintain, review, analyze, and regularly summarize quantitative and qualitative survey data on virtual events, regularly reporting on results and ensuring relevant constituent-generated content is accessible cross-departmentally. 7. Define, track, and report on metrics; analyze results; and devise strategies to consistently achieve growth in followers, impressions, engagement, and conversions.8. Other duties as assigned.  Benefits:American Bird Conservancy fosters a mission-driven, collaborative, and supportive workplace where staff are respected, empowered, and connected by a shared purpose. ABC offers strong benefits, a 35-hour work week, generous leave policies, and flexible, remote-friendly work arrangements that support a healthy work-life balance.Excellent benefits package includes medical, dental, flexible spending accounts, and employer-matched retirement savings, flexible schedule, etc.Paid vacation days (starting at 15 days annually and increasing to 20 days after 1 year of employment), paid holidays (including both US holidays and additional ABC holidays) and sick leave. Lodging, meals, and transportation covered for work-related travel away from home where applicable.Rental vehicle or personal mileage reimbursement where applicable.  To Apply:Please apply online at Paylocity with your resume, cover letter, and 3 writing samples and/or portfolio.If you have any difficulties uploading your resume, cover letter, and 3 writing samples and/or portfolio then please send them as ONE document to HR@abcbirds.org* ABC aligns our compensation with local market rates to ensure internal equity and fairness across our distributed workforce.At American Bird Conservancy, we believe that a diversity of backgrounds, perspectives, and skills makes us more effective. We are committed to creating a respectful and inclusive work environment for all of our employees. As an equal opportunity employer, ABC is committed to ensuring that employees and applicants for employment have equal opportunities regardless of race, color, national origin, ancestry, sex, age, religion, physical or mental disability, medical condition, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, or any other factor unrelated to the requirements of the position.

raw_description: About the Position More Perfect Union is expanding our coverage of sports stories reported through an economic justice lens. And videos from other channels we are inspired by: -- Why Saudi Arabia wants all the sports -- Architect Reveals How NFL Stadiums Are Engineered to Maximize Profit | WSJ Pro Perfected Ideal candidates will lead video projects from pitch to publish, overseeing all aspects of production, taking direction from senior More Perfect Union staff and working closely with other team members. Producers will be responsible for pitching and executing ideas related to sports that live within MPU’s mission of elevating working class issues and explaining complicated political and economic ideas to a broad audience through accessible YouTube formats. This role must be immersed in sports knowledge and excited about the process of telling those stories through video, with the technical and narrative skills to create powerful stories that hook viewers and keep people watching to the end. <p><strong>Who We Are:</strong></p> <p><strong>Being naked is the #1 most sustainable option. We're #2.</strong></p> <p>Since 2009, we've been on a mission to bring sustainable fashion to everyone. Named one of&nbs
raw_description: Who We Are:. Being naked is the #1 most sustainable option. We're #2. Since, we've been on a mission to bring sustainable fashion to everyone. Named one of Fast Company’s Brands That Matter and winning a Best Carbon Footprint award, we have big goals like being Climate Positive by, Circular by, and pushing the whole industry forward along the way. Our work has gotten love in Drapers and Sourcing Journal, and TIME wrote about how great our CEO, Hali Borenstein, is. Basically, we're saving the Earth and looking damn good doing it. We're a global brand with 50+ stores (and counting) around the world, and our own sustainable factory in LA. We innovate across categories like accessories, swimwear, and sleepwear, and we reach millions of people with campaigns like this, this and this. None of this work is possible without the incredible people behind it. We're a mission-based company that invests in an inclusive culture, so we can innovate together and ensure everyone has the space to grow, thrive, and belong. And starting right now, we want to do all that with you. Work Location/ Schedule: Onsite, 5 days - Vernon HQ. The Role: Quality Assurance Manager - Women’s Apparel. The Manager of Quality Assurance is responsible for driving product compliance and quality consistency across core QA processes — care label approvals, third-party inspection report review, fabric and garment testing, and high-return investigations for internationally produced product. This is a rep
description: The Manager of Quality Assurance is responsible for driving product compliance and quality consistency across core QA processes — care label approvals, third-party inspection report review, fabric and garment testing, and high-return investigations for internationally produced product. This is a report-driven role: you are managing quality outcomes mainly through analysis and vendor communication, not physical warehouse inspection. You are the key communication link between overseas vendors, third-party inspection partners, internal QC, and the Production, Tech Services, Fabric, and Merchandising teams. Special Call-Outs (~2%): Investigate escalated issues from Import Production, coordinate re-audits, and ensure vendors implement corrective actions to close out quality gaps. Candidates with warehouse or distribution QC backgrounds alone will not be considered.

Named one of Fast Company’s Brands That Matter and winning a Best Carbon Footprint award, we have big goals like being Climate Positive by, Circular by, and pushing the whole industry forward along the way. Our work has gotten love in Drapers and Sourcing Journal, and TIME wrote about how great our CEO, Hali Borenstein, is. Basically, we're saving the Earth and looking damn good doing it. We're a global brand with 50+ stores (and counting) around the world, and our own sustainable factory in LA. We innovate across categories like accessories, swimwear, and sleepwear, and we reach millions of people with campaigns like this, this and this. None of this work is possible without the incredible people behind it. We're a mission-based company that invests in an inclusive culture, so we can innovate together and ensure everyone has the space to grow, thrive, and belong. And starting right now, we want to do all that with you. The Role:. Assistant Manager, Retail Operations:. The Assistant Manager of Retail Operations reports to the Store Manager and is responsible for executing operational standards in their Reformation store location. The Assistant Manager of Retail Operations will help standardize processes and execute strategies for project management, budget management, and internal reporting. They will roll-up their sleeves to help drive real, sustainable implementation. The As
description: The Assistant Manager of Retail Operations reports to the Store Manager and is responsible for executing operational standards in their Reformation store location. The Assistant Manager of Retail Operations will help standardize processes and execute strategies for project management, budget management, and internal reporting. The Assistant Manager of Retail Operations Manager is a big picture thinker and a hands-on doer; is smart, adaptable, collaborative, and comfortable working with all levels in the store and HQ Leadership. This is a great role for a motivated self-starter looking to gain a deep understanding of a vertically integrated fashion business and managing the challenges that come from a quickly growing company. Implements, maintains and continuously manages suitable tools and metrics to support project effectiveness while aligning with the growing and scaling needs of Refor

raw_description: About GoodLeap: GoodLeap is a technology company delivering best-in-class financing and software products for sustainable solutions, from solar panels and batteries to energy-efficient HVAC, heat pumps, roofing, windows, and more. Over 1 million homeowners have benefited from our simple, fast, and frictionless technology that makes the adoption of these products more affordable, accessible, and easier to understand. Thousands of professionals deploying home efficiency and solar solutions rely on GoodLeap’s proprietary, AI-powered applications and developer tools to drive more transparent customer communication, deeper business intelligence, and streamlined payment and operations. Our platform has led to more than $30 billion in financing for sustainable solutions since. GoodLeap is also proud to support our award-winning nonprofit, GivePower, which is building and deploying life-saving water and clean electricity systems, changing the lives of more than 1.6 million people across Africa, Asia, and South America. We're looking for a Principal Product Manager to drive the fraud and risk product function for our consumer products. This is a high-impact IC role responsible for architecting the systems, controls, and decisioning that protect our credit products, consumer payments, and homeowner experiences while maintaining a seamless, high-conversion user journey. You'll own the strategy and execution for defined fraud and risk domains, bringing clarity to complex challenges,

raw_description: Qui sommes-nous?. Reformation, fondée en, est une marque révolutionnaire qui prouve que mode et développement durable peuvent coexister. Inspirées du vintage et conçues selon des pratiques durables, nos pièces en édition limitée s'adressent aux personnes qui souhaitent allier style et écoresponsabilité. Reformation est à la pointe de l’innovation en matière de mode durable et fait figure d’exemple dans l’industrie: elle gère la première manufacture durable de Los Angeles, utilise des tissus invendus et écologiques, suit et partage l’impact environnemental de ses produits, et investit dans les personnes qui rendent cette révolution possible. La marque a également innové dans le commerce de détail, en créant une expérience technologique en boutique qui combine le meilleur du shopping en ligne avec le plaisir d'acheter en personne. Notre entreprise est tournée vers l'avenir, et convaincue que les problèmes auxquels notre planète et notre société fontpeuvent être résolus grâce à l’innovation, le bon sens et l’éducation. Ne rien porter est l'option la plus durable. Nous sommes la 2ème. Chez Ref, les personnes passent avant tout le reste. Nous savons qu'il est impossible d'innover et de créer des trucs cool si tout le monde n'est pas impliqué. C'est pourquoi favoriser une culture où tout le monde se sent à sa place et où chaque voix compte vraiment est essentiel. Ensemble, nous construisons une culture inclusive tout en ayant un impact concret. Superviseur | euse des ven

raw_description: Who We Are:. Being naked is the #1 most sustainable option. We're #2. Since, we've been on a mission to bring sustainable fashion to everyone. Named one of Fast Company’s Brands That Matter and winning a Best Carbon Footprint award, we have big goals like being Climate Positive by, Circular by, and pushing the whole industry forward along the way. Our work has gotten love in Drapers and Sourcing Journal, and TIME wrote about how great our CEO, Hali Borenstein, is. Basically, we're saving the Earth and looking damn good doing it. We're a global brand with 50+ stores (and counting) around the world, and our own sustainable factory in LA. We innovate across categories like accessories, swimwear, and sleepwear, and we reach millions of people with campaigns like this, this, and this. None of this work is possible without the incredible people behind it. We're a mission-based company that invests in an inclusive culture, so we can innovate together and ensure everyone has the space to grow, thrive, and belong. And starting right now, we want to do all that with you. What You’ll Do:. Provide consistently magical customer service across all customer service channels. Deliver an excellent customer experience through a customer-first mindset to build long-term, loyal client relationships. Consistently meet and exceed performance goals. Be able to wear many hats (stylist, friend, personal assistant) when helping our customers shop for their favorite item. Know our shippi

About GoodLeap: GoodLeap is a technology company delivering best-in-class financing and software products for sustainable solutions, from solar panels and batteries to energy-efficient HVAC, heat pumps, roofing, windows, and more. Over 1 million homeowners have benefited from our
raw_description: About GoodLeap: GoodLeap is a technology company delivering best-in-class financing and software products for sustainable solutions, from solar panels and batteries to energy-efficient HVAC, heat pumps, roofing, windows, and more. Over 1 million homeowners have benefited from our simple, fast, and frictionless technology that makes the adoption of these products more affordable, accessible, and easier to understand. Thousands of professionals deploying home efficiency and solar solutions rely on GoodLeap’s proprietary, AI-powered applications and developer tools to drive more transparent customer communication, deeper business intelligence, and streamlined payment and operations. Our platform has led to more than $30 billion in financing for sustainable solutions since. GoodLeap is also proud to support our award-winning nonprofit, GivePower, which is building and deploying life-saving water and clean electricity systems, changing the lives of more than 1.6 million people across Africa, Asia, and South America. We're looking for a Senior Director of Brand Marketing & Communications to lead a cross functional marketing and communications function and connect it directly to business growth. This role requires someone who operates fluently in two directions: with executives and business leaders, you're the person who connects brand to business—bringing audience insight, storytelling, and digital experience into rooms where the conversation is about revenue, OKRs, and growth

raw_description: Who we are:. Since, we've been on a mission to bring sustainable fashion to everyone. Named one of Fast Company’s Brands That Matter and winning a Best Carbon Footprint award, we have big goals like being Climate Positive by, Circular by, and pushing the whole industry forward along the way. Our work has gotten love in Drapers and Sourcing Journal, and TIME wrote about how great our CEO, Hali Borenstein, is. Basically, we're saving the Earth and looking damn good doing it. We're a global brand with 50+ stores (and counting) around the world, and our own sustainable factory in LA. We innovate across categories like accessories, swimwear, and sleepwear, and we reach millions of people with campaigns like this, this and this. None of this work is possible without the incredible people behind it. We're a mission-based company that invests in an inclusive culture, so we can innovate together and ensure everyone has the space to grow, thrive, and belong. And starting right now, we want to do all that with you. Work Location/ Schedule:. Available to work a minimum of 30 hours per week, but up to 40 hours per week. Daily open availability across a full 7 day work week. Able to work flexible schedule (days, nights, weekends, & holidays if required to meet the needs. Full Time Sales Lead. At Reformation, the sales lead is an elite sales professional and a central figure in the Reformation store ecosystem. This is an elevated, commission-based role designed for a high-per
description: At Reformation, the sales lead is an elite sales professional and a central figure in the Reformation store ecosystem. This is an elevated, commission-based role designed for a high-performing entrepreneur who views retail as a career. You are a Wardrobe Consultant, responsible for managing a private book of business, driving exceptional SPH, and acting as a trend authority. Reporting to the Store Manager, essential include but are not limited to:. Serve as a trusted keyholder responsible for opening and closing of the store, ensuring all operations, and visual standards are flawlessly executed.

raw_description: About GoodLeap: GoodLeap is a technology company delivering best-in-class financing and software products for sustainable solutions, from solar panels and batteries to energy-efficient HVAC, heat pumps, roofing, windows, and more. Over 1 million homeowners have benefited from our simple, fast, and frictionless technology that makes the adoption of these products more affordable, accessible, and easier to understand. Thousands of professionals deploying home efficiency and solar solutions rely on GoodLeap’s proprietary, AI-powered applications and developer tools to drive more transparent customer communication, deeper business intelligence, and streamlined payment and operations. Our platform has led to more than $30 billion in financing for sustainable solutions since. GoodLeap is also proud to support our award-winning nonprofit, GivePower, which is building and deploying life-saving water and clean electricity systems, changing the lives of more than 1.6 million people across Africa, Asia, and South America. Position Overview We are seeking a highly skilled Senior Full-Stack Software Engineer to bring technical excellence, initiative and leadership to our team. As a Senior Engineer, you’ll be a key contributor to the design, development, and scaling of our platform, working closely with product managers, designers, and fellow engineers to build solutions that are secure, reliable, and performance-optimized. raw_description: Work Location/ Schedule: Vernon, CA Full-Time On-Site, Flexible to work various shifts as needed. Our Warehouse Lead possesses excellent problem-solving, communication, and leadership skills. You will be responsible for overseeing Leads and the production, quality, and safety of our Outbound and Inbound Departments. What You’ll Do:. Lead a team of 3 to 20 associates throughout the year and during sale seasons. Review and assign team to their duties according to the WLA. Monitor productivity of team and update UPH charts every hour to ensure goals are being met. Ensure Department SLAs are being met. Account for all equipment and make sure they are in working condition. Audit team to ensure they are following the correct procedures and meeting quality standards. Ensure all safety rules are strictly followed. Track employee-related errors and coach whenever necessary. Ensure backlogs are under control. Investigate and resolve any process discrepancies and problem-solving errors. Track and properly dispose of any hazardous materials. Foster and maintain good communication with other department leads. Maintain a neat, clean, and organized work area. Track department supplies and coordinate with the Inventory Team for restocks. Maintain good communication with management by communicating issues that arise during the shift. Responsible for completing Start-of-Shift (SOS) and -Shift (EOS) checklists. Complete EOS reports and speak to any misses within your department. Who You Are. 1+

raw_description: Wir sind nicht der lokale Stromanbieter um die Ecke. Wir sind das globale EnerTech-Unternehmen, das den Energiemarkt revolutionieren wird. Wir haben kein geringeres Ziel, als die global benötigte Energie % nachhaltig zu gestalten. Global, agil und zukunftsorientiert verändern wir den Energiemarkt mit neuer Technologie zum Besseren. Dabei sind unsere Ökostromtarife nur die Basis. Bereits seitinstallieren wir Wärmepumpen-Komplettlösungen mit eigenen Fachkräften. Außerdem arbeiten wir aktiv an der flächendeckenden Einführung von Smart Metern, Elektromobilitätslösungen und der eigenen Stromerzeugung aus Wind- und Sonnenenergie. Das schaffen wir allerdings nicht alleine, sondern dazu brauchen wir dich! Werde Teil unseres Teams und fordere den Status Quo heraus. Mit Energie, die gut für den Planeten ist und gut für dein Karma. 💚 Finde jetzt deinen Traumjob und werde Teil der Energierevolution von Octopus Energy Germany. Worum es bei der Rolle geht Datenschutz ist für uns kein reines Compliance-Thema. Er ist ein zentraler Bestandteil von gutem Kundenservice. Wir wachsen schnell, arbeiten datengetrieben und stehen täglich in engem Kontakt mit unseren Kundinnen und Kunden.ür brauchen wir eine Person, die Datenschutz operativ denkt, pragmatisch umsetzt und im Alltag verankert. title: Chief Research Officer
pay_candidate_snippets: Current Openings Chief Research Officer Remote Digital Advertising Associate Remote Director of Client Services Remote Lifecycle Marketing Manager Remote Manager, Membership Mobilization Remote Managing Director Remote

Current Openings. Chief Research Officer. Remote.. Digital Advertising Associate. Remote.. Director of Client Services. Remote.. Lifecycle Marketing Manager. Remote.. Manager, Membership Mobilization. Remote.. Managing Director. Remote.. Chief Research Officer https://goodpower.a
Chief Research Officer
raw_description: Skip To Job Description Help us build a better economy that works for everyone GoodPower is at an exciting, pivotal moment as we launch our newStrategic Plan. Between now and the end of the, we are leveling up all areas of our work, and plan to grow our organization by 5x to meet this critical moment with boldness and excellence. We are seeking talented, professional, creative and highly skilled teammates who are are energized by a dynamic, fast-paced work environment of continuous growth. At GoodPower, we love our jobs, our mission, and what we can do together. GoodPower offers an excellent overall compensation package which includes a competitive base salary commensurate with skills and experience; 95% employer-paid medical, dental, and vision insurance, HSA, and FSA plans;retirement savings plan with matching company contributions; paid vacation, sick leave, floating holidays, volunteer days and 13 paid holidays; monthly internet and wellness stipend; and paid parental leave and sabbatical program. GoodPower is an equal opportunity employer that deeply values diversity, equity, and inclusion. We strongly encourage applications from people of color, Indigenous people, individuals who experience gender oppression, people with disabilities, and members of the LGBTQIA+ community. Chief Research Officer Remote Full Time Executive Share Remote-based in the US   Overview GoodPower is a global organization working to unlock the economic potential of decarbonization through research, innovation, strategic communications, and disruptive technology. We focus on advancing more affordable and reliable energy, expanding economic opportunity, strengthening energy security, supporting healthier and more resilient communities, and accelerating the transition to a predictable, secure, modern low-carbon economy. Operating at the intersection of data, digital media, emerging technology, behavioral research, and public engagement, GoodPower develops and deploys scalable strategies designed to accelerate the adoption of decarbonized technologies and infrastructure—including renewable energy, regenerative agriculture, energy storage, and electric transportation. Our work combines rigorous research and experimentation with advanced communications and innovation capabilities to better understand public attitudes, test effective interventions, and help build the social, economic, and market conditions necessary for large-scale transformation. GoodPower is at a pivotal stage of organizational growth as we approach completion of the first year of ourStrategic Plan. Over the next five years, we intend to significantly expand our reach, capabilities, and impact—investing deeply in innovation, AI-enabled systems, data and analytics, research and development, and next-generation communications infrastructure to help accelerate the global energy transition and the economic opportunities it creates. Job Summary GoodPower is an evidence-based, nimble, and adaptive organization that routinely evaluates our programs to measure impact. Over the last, we have continually tested and refined our approach, and have successfully built a set of program offerings that blend cost-efficiency, rigor, scale, and measurable impact. Our programs have demonstrated success through external evaluation, including with partners at Stanford, Yale, and George Mason Universities. We also collaborate regularly with research partners such as Analyst Institute, GrowProgress, One Minus Beta, and Tufts University’s Center for Information Research on Civic Learning and Engagement (CIRCLE). The Chief Research Officer (CRO) will direct GoodPower’s Good Data Lab, our in-house empirical research lab. The CRO will work internally with staff, in collaboration with partners, and alongside vendors to design, launch and implement research studies with the goal of understanding how to effectively communicate and increasing GoodPower’s efficacy across a wide variety of key impact areas, including: issue awareness and persuasion, issue and advocacy engagement, attitude and behavior change, civic participation. The CRO will serve as key public representative of the organization providing internal and external thought leadership. The CRO oversees in-house research, experimentation, and evaluation, including but not limited to: message-testing experiments with diverse audiences in rural, urban, and international geographies on a variety of energy, economic, and political issues, randomized control trials to increase the impact of our voter registration and GOTV programs, evaluations of programs to determine if they are ready for scale, and surveys and focus groups to understand the perspectives and motivations of key audience segment.  This position reports to the Chief Executive Officer. Travel will be required to departmental meetings, staff retreats, and other events to represent the department and organization.   Key Responsibilities Program mission and strategy: Develop and lead research and experimentation strategy and framework to generate evidence for the most effective ways to leverage GoodPower strengths to advance our mission, vision and programmatic goals. Program management: Manage the execution of multiple simultaneous projects to support GoodPower’s key program areas of public education, storytelling, organizing, advocacy, civic engagement, and digital marketing. This may occur with in-house staff or with partners, vendors, and contractors. Manage departmental operation and budget. Staff management: Manage Senior Researcher, Senior Research Analyst, and Senior Research Manager and support management of research staff and vendors.  Grant development: Support the development of research proposals and/or research designs within funding proposals.  Cross-department collaboration : Facilitate inter-team communication and collaboration to ensure different program areas are aligned and working together. Organizational leadership: Create a culture of shared vision, mutual accountability and individual ownership over work within the GoodPower team.  Research and experimental design: Design research projects, experiments, and evaluative tools to measure the impact and efficacy of GoodPower’s programs. Sharing results and learnings: Analyze and synthesize results of evaluations and experiments and disseminate results through written communications and presentations for all audiences, including funders and donors, board members, staff, policymakers and partners. Represent GoodPower’s Good Data Lab in coalition and partner spaces. Stay updated: Stay abreast of research findings from partners, peer organizations, and the community, including by attending online and live events and conferences. Increase brand recognition: Help to establish GoodPower as a visible leader in data-driven impact in the fields of climate and renewable energy education, advocacy, civic engagement, and democracy. Manage public-facing research reputation and the reputation of the Good Data Lab.   Qualifications A PhD in research, social sciences, communication, psychology, measurement and evaluation, statistics or similar field is required.  15+  years of experience in research, experimentation and evaluation. Previous organizational  leadership and management experience, including staff management. Knowledge of both qualitative and quantitative research and evaluation methods. Extensive experience with statistical software such as SQL, STATA, R, SAS, SPSS, or others. Excellent oral and written communication skills in English for technical and nontechnical audiences, and experience with public speaking. Proven experience presenting information to Boards of Directors, funders, partners and cross-functional staff teams. Experience with grant application and management, and budgeting. Highly proficient in modern collaboration and project management tools (Google Workspace, Slack, Jira, Salesforce).   Position Details Annual salary range: $175,000 - $205,000, commensurate with experience Generous benefits include: Medical, Dental, Vision,retirement savings plan, Vacation, Sabbatical, Paid Parental Leave, 2 Floating Holidays, 2 Community Service Floating Holidays, sick time, two weeks of full-staff time off (July 4 week and Christmas-New Years week) and 13 observed holidays GoodPower is an equal-opportunity employer that highly values staff diversity Location: Remote This description reflects GoodPower’s assignment of essential functions; it does not restrict the tasks that may be assigned. GoodPower retains the right to change or assign other duties to this position at any time. Employees must be able to perform the essential functions of the position satisfactorily. Reasonable accommodations will be made to enable employees with disabilities to perform the essential functions of their job, absent undue hardship. GoodPower has a zero-tolerance standard for abuse and inappropriate behavior by staff members.    GoodPower is an equal-opportunity employer that highly values diversity, equity, and inclusion and views the climate crisis as a social justice crisis. People who identify as Black, Indigenous, and People of Color (BIPOC), people who experience gender oppression, people with disabilities, and people who identify as members of the LGBTQIA+ community are particularly encouraged to apply.       Share Apply Apply for this position Required * First Name * Last Name * Email Address * Phone * Address Resume * We've received your resume. Click here to update it. Attach resume or Paste resume Attach resume as.pdf,.doc,.docx,.odt,.txt, or.rtf (limit 5MB) or Paste resume Paste your resume here or Attach resume file Cover Letter * Desired salary * Earliest start date? Do you live in the United States? * -- No answer -- Yes No, I'm willing to relocate No and I'm not willing to relocate Do you have a PhD in research, social sciences, communication, measurement and evaluation, statistics or similar field field? If so, which one? * -- No answer -- Social Science Communications Measurement and Evaluation Statistics Similar Field Non-related field How many years of experience in research, experimentation and evaluation? (Include experience gained during PhD program) * -- No answer -- Less than 5 years 5 - 10 years 10 - 15 years More than 15 years Human Check * Submit Application

Digital Advertising Associate Remote Full Time Mid Level Share Remote-based in the US   Overview GoodPower works globally to unlock the enormous economic potential of the energy transition—more affordable energy bills, better and more abundant jobs, healthier food, economic security for families and farmers, and a better economy that works for all of us. We operate at the intersection of digital media, smart tech, civic participation, and advocacy to reach millions of people annually with our work to: lower costs and create jobs, shift culture to transform beliefs and behaviors, and accelerate the deployment of decarbonized technologies like: renewables, regenerative agriculture and electric vehicles—one individual, one neighborhood, one community at a time.   GoodPower is at an exciting, pivotal moment as we launch our new strategic plan throughto level up all areas of our work–growing our organization 5x over the next five years and relentlessly honing our skills and expertise to be the best that we can be to transform our renewable energy economy.   Job Summary In this position, the Digital Advertising Associate will work to advance the programmatic and strategic growth of GoodPower through implementing, managing, and improving GoodPower’s paid advertising campaign strategies in key states and nationally. They will work to strengthen GoodPower’s role and impact in advocating for an end to the era of fossil fuels, a swift and equitable transition to renewable energy, and an increase in diverse youth participation in our democracy as well as devise creative strategies to inoculate persuadable audiences for GoodPower’s current and future white label entities.    This is a future-facing role for an ads expert who thrives at the convergence of digital innovation, audience segmentation, and data-driven campaign performance. This is a full-time, remote position based in the United States. The  Digital Advertising Associate will work from home, meet regularly by video chat with GoodPower team members and partners, and conduct the majority of activities both online and in-person, when safe. The  Digital Advertising Associate must be available for travel within the United States to participate in staff retreats and/or conferences.   Key Responsibilities Campaign Strategy Management Design, launch, and manage digital and traditional advertising campaigns across multiple platforms (Meta, Google Ads, YouTube, X, LinkedIn, Reddit, Twitch, audio, programmatic DSPs, and terrestrial media). Develop budgets and paid media plans; monitor performance to ensure delivery of key metrics. Manage audiences, targeting, creative assets, and tracking systems to optimize performance. Creative Development Optimization Write and refine ad copy; collaborate with vendors and staff to produce engaging creatives. Build and optimize action pages to drive list growth, conversions, and lower cost-per-lead. Leverage retargeting and segmentation to increase retention and campaign effectiveness. Implement new tracking and reporting methods to enhance performance insights. Cross-Departmental Collaboration Partner with program and campaign teams to align advertising with organizational priorities (e.g., voter registration, siting permitting, clean energy acceleration, IRA defense, workforce development, and white-label initiatives). Support the development team with funder collateral, including campaign impact reports and presentations. Coordinate with communications and compliance teams to ensure proper C3/C4 activity reporting. Data, Insights Innovation Monitor and analyze KPIs; provide actionable recommendations for optimization. Research and test innovative strategies to expand reach, engagement, and efficiency. Stay current on audience sentiment, platform innovations, and industry trends   Qualifications   Minimum Qualifications 3 plus years of experience in the digital marketing or digital organizing field Strong analytical skills with the ability to set priorities, and complete work and meet deadlines with minimal supervision Strong Excel or Google Sheets skills Self-starter with strong organizational skills and creativity and can take big-picture strategy and couple it with strategic implementation. GSuite, Slack, Canva, Jira, etc.)   Desired Qualifications Experience building, managing, and evaluating paid advertising campaigns within Facebook Ads Manager and Google Ads Experience working with Google Tag Manager, Facebook Pixel, and other event-tracking mechanisms Copywriting experience Graphic design experience   This description reflects GoodPower’s assignment of essential functions; it does not restrict the tasks that may be assigned. Director of Client Services Remote Full Time Senior Manager/Supervisor Share Remote-based in the US   Overview GoodPower works globally to unlock the enormous economic potential of the energy transition—more affordable energy bills, better and more abundant jobs, healthier food, economic security for families and farmers, and a better economy that works for all of us. We operate at the intersection of digital media, smart tech, civic participation, and advocacy to reach millions of people annually with our work to: lower costs and create jobs, shift culture to transform beliefs and behaviors, and accelerate the deployment of decarbonized technologies like: renewables, regenerative agriculture and electric vehicles—one individual, one neighborhood, one community at a time. GoodPower is at an exciting, pivotal moment as we launch our new strategic plan throughto level up all areas of our work–growing our organization 5x over the next five years and relentlessly honing our skills and expertise to be the best that we can be to transform our renewable energy economy.   Job Summary In this position, the Director of Client Services will play a central role in advancing GoodPower’s mission by building and leading high-impact partnerships with the organizations, campaigns, and communities we serve. This leader will oversee the full client lifecycle, from onboarding through ongoing engagement and expansion, ensuring every partner experiences the depth of GoodPower’s expertise and the transformative potential of our work. The Director of Client Services will serve as a strategic connector between GoodPower’s internal teams and its external partners, ensuring campaigns are delivered with excellence, relationships are nurtured with care, and outcomes are measured and communicated with clarity. This role is a key driver of GoodPower’s growth trajectory as we scale to meet the demands of an accelerating energy transition. This is a full-time, remote position based in the United States. The Director of Client Services will work from home, meet regularly by video chat with GoodPower team members and partners, and conduct the majority of activities both online and in-person, when needed. The Director of Client Services must be available for travel within the United States, to participate in staff retreats, client meetings, and/or conferences. Key Responsibilities Serve as the primary strategic point of contact for a portfolio of key client accounts, cultivating trusted, long-term relationships with partner organizations, advocates, and campaigns across our client base. Lead client onboarding processes, ensuring partners are set up for success from day one with clear expectations, defined success metrics, and a deep understanding of GoodPower’s capabilities and approach. Collaborate cross-functionally with GoodPower’s paid media, digital strategy, creative, and data teams to ensure client programs are executed with precision and delivered on time and within scope. Develop and present regular performance reports and strategic recommendations to clients, translating campaign data into actionable insights that drive impact and strengthen the partnership. Identify and pursue opportunities to deepen and expand client engagements, working closely with leadership to develop proposals, scope new work, and grow GoodPower’s portfolio of mission-aligned partners. Maintain a strong pulse on the policy landscape, advocacy ecosystem, and clean energy sector to bring fresh, relevant context to client conversations and campaign planning. Support the development of systems, processes, and tools that enable the client services function to scale efficiently as GoodPower grows toward itsstrategic goals. Represent GoodPower with professionalism and passion at external events, partner convenings, and industry conferences.   Qualifications Minimum Qualifications 5+ years of experience in client services, account management, partnership development, or a closely related field, preferably within digital advocacy, nonprofit communications, or the clean energy sector. Demonstrated ability to manage multiple complex client relationships simultaneously with strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to meet deadlines with minimal supervision. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to synthesize complex information and present it clearly to a diverse range of stakeholders. Strong analytical mindset with experience reviewing campaign performance data and translating it into strategic recommendations. Collaborative, solutions-oriented approach with the ability to work effectively across internal teams and with external partners. Familiarity with digital tools (e.g., GSuite, Slack, CRM platforms, etc.)   Desired Qualifications Experience working in or alongside climate, clean energy, environmental justice, or advocacy organizations. Background in digital marketing or integrated communications campaigns. Proven track record of growing client accounts and contributing to revenue or partnership development goals. Experience building or scaling a client services or account management function within a growing organization.   This description reflects GoodPower’s assignment of essential functions; it does not restrict the tasks that may be assigned. GoodPower retains the right to change or assign other duties to this position at any time. Employees must be able to perform the essential functions of the position satisfactorily. Reasonable accommodations will be made to enable employees with disabilities to perform the essential functions of their job, absent undue hardship. GoodPower has a zero-tolerance standard for abuse and inappropriate behavior by staff members.   Position Details Annual salary range: $125,000-135,000, commensurate with experience Generous benefits include: Medical, Dental, Vision,retirement savings plan, Vacation, Sabbatical, Paid Parental Leave, 2 Floating Holidays, 2 Community Service Floating Holidays, sick time, two weeks of full-staff time off (July 4 week and Christmas-New Years week) and 13 observed holidays GoodPower is an equal-opportunity employer that highly values staff diversity Location: Remote   Application Guidelines GoodPower is an equal-opportunity employer that highly values diversity, equity, and inclusion and views the climate crisis as a social justice crisis. People who identify as Black, Indigenous, and People of Color (BIPOC), people who experience gender oppression, people with disabilities, and people who identify as members of the LGBTQIA+ community are particularly encouraged to apply.   Share Apply Apply for this position Required * First Name * Last Name * Email Address * Phone * Address Resume * We've received your resume. Manager, Membership Mobilization Remote Full Time Mid Level Share Remote-based in the US Overview GoodPower works to unlock the enormous economic potential of the energy transition—more affordable energy bills, better and more abundant jobs, healthier food, economic security for families and farmers, and a better economy that works for all of us. We operate at the intersection of digital media, smart tech, elections, and renewable energy advocacy. We aim to reach millions of people annually with our work to: lower costs and create jobs, shift culture to transform beliefs and behaviors, and accelerate the deployment of decarbonized technologies. At GoodPower, we are building a team of talented, driven doers who thrive in fast-paced, mission-oriented environments. We value problem solvers who combine strategic thinking with an orientation toward action. We seek collaborative leaders who can inspire and develop others. Our people are eager to innovate, adapt, and scale efforts that can transform the renewable energy economy and strengthen democracy.   Job Summary The Membership Mobilization Manager works to advance GoodPower’s work to accelerate a renewable energy economy through building a membership engagement program across GoodPower and its independent sub-brands who are primed to take high level actions from attending and organizing events in their communities, mobilizing others, calling and writing to elected leaders, submitting public comments, testifying before government bodies, writing op-eds and letters to the editor, engaging in their communities, and sharing their stories.  The Manager spearheads the membership and in-list communications plan, rooted in deep understanding of the various audiences and what most effectively motivates action, how to leverage members to take online and offline actions, and tailors strategies, messages, and action opportunities for each audience to advance GoodPower’s mission and goals.    This is a full time, remote position based anywhere in the United States. The Membership Mobilization Manager will work from home, meet regularly by video chat with GoodPower team members, partners and youth, and conduct activities both online and in-person. The Membership Mobilization Manager must be available for regular travel within the United States to participate in conferences, select actions, events, and to attend staff retreats. This position reports to the Head of Policy and Public Relations.   Key Responsibilities Planning Implementation Leads the launch of a new membership program, by spearheading the planning and execution of GoodPower’s and independent subbrands' membership and in-list communications strategy for regular engagement of audiences through action opportunities, wins and successes, and other interventions. Develops innovative approaches to and implements proven strategies to generate online and offline opportunities.  Collaborates with the National Field Director and State Field Directors in each state to help develop and implement strategic, coordinated organizing plans to engage members around on-the-ground events and activations for issue campaigns to accelerate the renewable energy transition and electoral campaigns, including canvasses, volunteer recruitment, phonebanks and other mobilizations. Collaborates with the National Political Director around key races and opportunities to mobilize voters up and down the ballot. Develop and run the member-to-member electoral field campaign. Collaborates with the Lifecycle Marketing Manager (TBH) to leverage automations, flows and regular communications to move members up a ladder of engagement.  Collaborates with the Data Analytics Director, Research Experimentation and Digital Data teams for deep analysis of the current membership audiences and what messages motivate them to act and build testing and experiments into all communications.  Ensure that all field activities are properly tracked in VAN and metrics and reports are developed for all program activity in collaboration with the Data Analytics Director and National Field Director. Ensure compliance and reporting of all membership related activities working with the Compliance Manager, finance team and legal experts. Collaborates with the Communications Director on an annual and monthly communications calendar with strategic opportunities for intervention across geographies and brands that is regularly updated based on current events, inflection points and other timely engagement opportunities. Develops plan to share wins, successes and stories across membership and in-list audiences to build a sense of self-efficacy. Connects communications with major movement moments, legislative cycles, news headlines, local activation opportunities and other breaking stories to help the organization best position its work. Communication Delivery Writing, styling, programming and sending communications via e-mail, SMS, and other communication channels to GoodPower and sub-brand members and in-list network about action opportunities, GoodPower successes, stories, or other needs as they arise. Conceptualizing and building engaging and strategic actions (e.g. petitions, email-to-target, attending events, etc.) including evergreen actions as well as one-off opportunities that advance members along a ladder of engagement in furtherance of GoodPower’s mission and values. Segmenting lists and tailoring messages to best resonate with each of the appropriate sub-audiences. Ensuring all in-list content aligns with the GoodPower's brand voice and values. Ensuring that communications, field, social media, advertising, and creator marketing groups are advised of any and all member and in-list communications and that plans are maximized with the inclusion of all marketing vehicles. Ensuring welcome flows are timely and relevant to each particular audience. Anticipating and mobilizing to quickly generate communications for rapid response opportunities as they arise. Analysis Collaborate with the Research Experimentation team as well as the Data Analytics Director to identify which messages are most effective at inspiring progressive levels of action. Through independent analysis and in collaboration with the Data team, identify which action opportunities generate the highest level of engagement. Other Duties as Assigned:   Adapt to the evolving needs of a fast-paced organization with flexibility and creativity.   Qualifications Experience 3-5+ years experience in membership engagement, campaigning, organizing, communications, advocacy or similar work, preferably in the political or renewable energy field. Skills Expertise Ability to build a new complex program, collaborating across departments, juggle and think through multiple angles, and ensure message discipline across communications. Prior experience running a successful membership mobilization program. Strong writing skills and ability to adapt tone based on audiences, key messages, and communication vehicles while maintaining message discipline Ability to prioritize multiple, competing opportunities. Experience writing communications (via email programs such as Engaging Networks, Twilio or mobile via MobileCommons). Competency in utilizing CRMs (such as Salesforce) and digital campaigning tools (VAN, Thrutext / Thrutalk, Mobilize and others). Awareness of and ability to communicate the economic impacts,  drivers and motivators of the renewable energy transition. Ability to communicate across audiences from a diversity of demographics and across the political spectrum–whether talking to rural neighbors in Wisconsin or young voters on a college campus in Pennsylvania. Strong analytical skills with ability to set priorities, complete work with minimal supervision, and meet deadlines. Attributes Self-starter with strong organizational skills and creativity. Ability to work independently and as a member of various teams. Demonstrated competence in managing complex projects. Team player who is a strong cross-team collaborator and communicator with an eagerness to jump into other duties than assigned. Proactive, flexible, and solutions-oriented with a commitment to innovation.   Compensation Annual salary range: $78,000-$83,000 commensurate with experience. Generous benefits include: Medical, Dental, Vision,retirement savings plan, Vacation, Sabbatical, Paid Parental Leave, 2 Floating Holidays, 2 Community Service Floating Holidays, sick time, two weeks of full-staff time off (July 4 week and Christmas-New Years week) and 13 observed holidays. GoodPower is an equal-opportunity employer that highly values staff diversity.   This description reflects GoodPower’s assignment of essential functions; it does not restrict the tasks that may be assigned. GoodPower retains the right to change or assign other duties to this position at any time. Employees must be able to perform the essential functions of the position satisfactorily. Managing Director Remote Full Time Executive Share Remote-based in the US   Overview GoodPower exists to accelerate the world’s transition to a decarbonized economy—more affordable energy bills, better and more abundant jobs, healthier food, economic security for families and farmers, and a better economy that works for all of us. We operate at the intersection of digital media, smart tech, civic participation, and advocacy to reach millions of people annually with our work to: lower costs and create jobs, shift culture to transform beliefs and behaviors, and accelerate the deployment of decarbonized technologies like: utility-scale renewables, regenerative agriculture and electric vehicles—one individual, one neighborhood, one community at a time.   GoodPower is at an exciting, pivotal moment as we launch our new strategic plan throughto level up all areas of our work–growing our organization 5x over the next five years and relentlessly honing our skills and expertise to be the best that we can be to transform our renewable energy economy.   Job Summary The Managing Director is the CEO’s primary partner and the connective tissue of the organization—ensuring GoodPower operates with clarity, cohesion, and disciplined execution. Acting as an extension of the CEO, this leader drives alignment across teams, strengthens internal systems, and ensures that GoodPower’s priorities translate into measurable impact.   This position is ideal for a mission-driven, highly detail-oriented executive who excels at navigating complexity, anticipating needs, and creating the conditions for the CEO and executive leadership team to perform at their highest level. The right candidate possesses exceptional interpersonal and communication skills, builds trust, motivates teams, and moves work forward with grace and influence.   Key Responsibilities Executive Leadership and Partnership Serve as the CEO’s right hand—supporting planning, decision-making, and organizational priority-setting. Act as a trusted thought partner to the CEO, offering strong judgment, insight, and strategic clarity. Represent the CEO in meetings and decision-making settings when requested, ensuring continuity of leadership and expectations. Work with the Executive Assistant to the CEO to manage the CEO’s bandwidth by ensuring follow-through, clarity of priorities, and proactive problem-solving.   Organizational Alignment and Execution Ensure cross-departmental alignment to keep teams coordinated, informed, and moving in the same direction. Translate goals into clear work plans, timelines, and organizational rhythms that keep leaders accountable. Surface organizational bottlenecks or conflicts early and develop solutions that keep teams aligned and supported. Drive internal communication systems that foster transparency, collaboration, and shared understanding. Be the ultimate stop-gap for programmatic and operational success.   Operational Excellence and Systems Leadership Identify operational challenges and design systems that help GoodPower operate more effectively and scale sustainably. Maintain high-level visibility into program performance, KPIs, and cross-team deliverables; support leaders in using data for decision-making. Partner closely with Operations, HR, and Finance to ensure program and organizational needs are supported with efficiency and compliance.   Leadership Support, Team Enablement and Culture Support coaching of senior program directors, helping them operate with clarity, strategic alignment, and strong execution. Build strong relationships across the organization; serve as a trusted connector who can hold people accountable while fostering respect and collaboration. Champion a culture grounded in communication, accountability, psychological safety, and continuous improvement. Model transparency, diplomacy, calm leadership, and discretion — especially in sensitive organizational conversations.   Board, Funder and Executive Communications Support the CEO in preparing and communicating materials for board meetings, funder briefings, investor conversations, external communications channels and high-stakes moments. Ensure seamless information flow to the CEO and executive team so decisions are grounded in clarity and context. Draft, review, or refine communications, executive updates, and organizational narratives as needed.   Qualifications 5+ years as a Deputy Director, Chief of Staff, Managing Director or CEO for a fast-paced organization spanning operations, program management, and executive support functions. Demonstrated success as a right-hand to a CEO, Executive Director, or C-suite leader. Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills—including the ability to build trust, deliver clear messaging, navigate sensitive dynamics, and influence without authority. Proven track record in organizational alignment, effective execution and managing cross-functional priorities in fast-moving environments. Strong systems thinker with experience designing and improving operational processes at scale. High emotional intelligence, diplomacy, and discretion; excels at managing relationships across levels and functions. Highly proficient in modern collaboration and project management tools (Google Workspace, Slack, Jira or Monday.com, Salesforce), and comfortable with data-driven decision-making. Experience leading or supporting distributed, remote teams is a plus. Thoughtful, steady, proactive, and grounded—able to bring clarity and confidence to ambiguity.   This description reflects GoodPower’s assignment of essential functions; it does not restrict the tasks that may be assigned. raw_description: At Octopus Energy Trading, we’re on a mission to reshape the future of energy. As part of Octopus Energy Group, we’re creating an innovative approach to trading that will accelerate the transition to a Net Zero world. With the growth of renewables and a push toward decarbonising heating and transport, greater flexibility in the grid is essential. We are building cutting-edge technology to optimise everything, from domestic EV charging to grid-scale batteries, to meet the global demand for energy flexibility. We’re looking for passionate and unconventional thinkers to join us on this journey, bringing a diversity of experience and ideas to shape a more efficient, flexible, and sustainable energy system. With an increase in variable and distributed supply, using data for trading and forecasting has never been more important. Octopus has always had a tech-first approach, and our trading and analytics tools are all built in-house by specialised domain teams, focussed on areas like grid-scale batteries or market fundamentals, all building on a common platform that is managed by our core engineering team. We are seeking a software engineer to join the core engineering team and help own the developer experience. You will be responsible for designing the frameworks and tooling that allow our domain teams to develop, ship, and manage event-driven microservices with zero overhead, as well as building and maintaining core microservices that can be re-used by domain teams. function: s can cross-functionally join forces and learn from one another
raw_description: Locations Collaboration is key at RWE. Our U.S. offices allow for an environment where colleagues can converse, create and drive forward change. With locations across the United States, we aim to create workplaces that nurture the needs of our employees and their teams. Our offices boast modern working spaces in exciting cities, where teams can cross-functionally join forces and learn from one another. Our offices at a glance Austin Bellevue (AI Lab) Chicago New York City Oakland San Diego Austin With its modern design and east-side location our Austin office offers all the charm that this capital city has to offer. Employees have access to on-site parking and a fitness center, fully stocked snack bar, complimentary soft drinks and plenty of recreational space; indoors and outdoors to spend time with fellow colleagues. Find jobs in Austin (rwe.com) Bellevue (AI Lab) In, we opened our RWE AI Research Laboratory in Bellevue, aiming to become a global leader in developing forecasting models. Why here? Because the Greater Seattle area is a growing hotspot for artificial intelligence and renewable energy. Bellevue brings together top tech talent, a strong research environment, a vibrant startup scene and many companies driving the energy transition - the perfect conditions to boost innovation and collaboration. Chicago RWE’s Chicago office is in the center of it all in the bustling city. Steps away from the scenic riverwalk, lively restaurants and multiple transportatio
description: Our offices boast modern working spaces in exciting cities, where teams can cross-functionally join forces and learn from one another. Steps away from the scenic riverwalk, lively restaurants and multiple transportation hubs (Union Station and Oglivie Transportation Center), our office is proud to be in a LEED Platinum and Energy Start-certified building. In order to support RWE’s ambitious expansion plans in North America, the New York-based team moved into a larger and more suitable office space within the Rockefeller Center in midtown Manhattan in spring. The new building is a LEED-certified high rise to Lake Merritt. All locations of RWE in the Americas Our offices boast modern working spaces in exciting cities, where teams can cross-functionally join forces and learn from one another.

title: Architect - (Mission Critical) - USA
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth. Position Overview. SNHA, A Woolpert Company is an award-winning global architectural practice, focused on performance-driven, highly complex facilities for some of the world’s largest technology clients. We blend applied research, strategic thinking, and technology-driven design methods, while seeking innovation that will define the future of our profession and drive solutions to the most complex design problems. We are seeking to hire an Architect to join our Mission Critical practice within our Buildings Team for our Chicago, IL, New York, NY, Pittsburgh and/or Miami, FL offices and offer a hybrid work schedule. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. We seek a highly motivated team member who is passionate about their work and fired by intellectual curiosity. We are looking for those who seek to change the way the built environment is imagined, designed, and constructed. Ideal candid

raw_description: Octopus Energy started with a bold idea: to build Britain’s first truly digital energy supplier. By combining world-class tech with brilliant humans who care deeply about customers and driving the renewable energy transition, we made it happen and in, we became Britain’s largest energy supplier. Now, we’re scaling fast and building the generation of products to accelerate the green energy transition. From making it effortless to switch to heat pumps, solar and EVs, to launching smart tariffs and creating renewable generation at scale, we’re solving some of the most complex challenges in energy with speed, creativity and customer obsession. We’re also enabling our global businesses to reach the full capability of what we’ve achieved in the UK, taking the learnings, products and experiences that customers love here and rolling them out worldwide. At Octopus Tech, you’ll have real ownership, variety, and the chance to shape products that make a tangible difference in people’s lives - lowering bills, enabling greener living, and delivering experiences customers genuinely love. You’ll work side by side with teams across marketing, operations, and data, and see the impact of your work in the real world, fast. We are looking for Senior Backend Engineers to join our Flexibility team. The flexibility team is building the generation of products and tools to make energy more flexible.
