raw_description: Help us make a big green dent in the universe. We're on a mission to drive the global renewable energy revolution, and we need loads more people to help us make our vision a reality. Octopus Energy Generation (OEGEN) is a leader in sourcing, investing in, and managing a wide range of utility-scale renewable energy assets. As we continue to expand our investment base, we are dedicated to building a diverse team that reflects the communities we serve. Since our entry into the renewables market in, OEGEN has achieved remarkable milestones, becoming a leading investor in onshore wind across mainland Europe, the largest investor of solar power in Europe, and a major investor into offshore wind projects. Currently, we manage a global portfolio of over £7 billion in renewable energy assets. Our operational portfolio consists of more than four hundred large-scale energy-generating companies. We take pride in our wind farms, solar power plants, and upcoming projects in energy storage and hydrogen. To support our ambitious growth plans and long-term ownership of energy assets, we are expanding our team. About the role We are looking for a Fund Asset Manager to drive asset performance across a range of renewable technologies and geographies. Working with both the asset management and fund management teams, the successful candidate will build a strong understanding of strategic priorities and use that knowledge to direct priorities accordingly. This role is highly execution-focused,
description: To support our ambitious growth plans and long-term ownership of energy assets, we are expanding our team. Working with both the asset management and fund management teams, the successful candidate will build a strong understanding of strategic priorities and use that knowledge to direct priorities accordingly. This role is highly execution-focused, with time split across issue management, risk mitigation, and performance optimisation. The successful candidate will be expected to leverage external partners where appropriate and maintain key supplier relationships The role will be based in London, with flexibility for remote working to support work-life balance. The role will report to the Fund Asset Director.

Octopus Energy, fournisseur d’énergie verte présent à l’international, a lancé sa filiale Octopus Energy Services pour accompagner ses clients dans la décarbonation complète de leur foyer. Déjà bien implantée au Royaume-Uni, cette entité propose un ensemble de solutions techniques: installation de panneaux photovoltaïques, de bornes de recharge pour véhicules électriques et de systèmes de chauffage décarboné, le tout avec une approche centrée sur la qualité, l’innovation et sans sous-traitance. Operations Data Analyst
raw_description: Benvenuti nel futuro dell’energia! La missione di Octopus Energy è rendere l’energia rinnovabile accessibile a tutti. Grazie alla tecnologia, stiamo guidando la rivoluzione energetica a livello globale, offrendo energia pulita a prezzi convenienti e un servizio clienti eccellente. Se non ti piacciono le sfide non inviarci la tua candidatura;) In Octopus Energy non amiamo la burocrazia, le regole troppo stringenti e le offerte commerciali poco chiare. Vogliamo rivoluzionare il mercato energetico italiano. Per farlo, siamo alla ricerca di persone motivate, che condividano i nostri stessi valori. Se hai a cuore il futuro del nostro pianeta e vuoi aiutarci a migliorare il settore energetico, unisciti al nostro team! Con la crescita vertiginosa che stiamo avendo in Octopus, è emersa la straordinaria opportunità di fare la differenza andando a utilizzare in modo intelligente e creativo la grande mole di dati che ogni giorno gestiamo come compagnia energetica. Per fare questo abbiamo quindi deciso di aprire una posizione come Operations Data Analyst. In questo ruolo potrai avere un impatto positivo sulla vita dei nostri clienti, sul pianeta e su chi ogni giorno aiuta direttamente i clienti. Infatti, in Octopus le Operations sono il cuore di tutto ciò che facciamo: persone stupende che ogni giorno aiutano i nostri clienti con un approccio universale e con l’autonomia di soddisfare le loro richieste con creatività e amore Se non ti piacciono le sfide non inviarci la tua candidatura;)

The ideal candidate will have a strong understanding of software quality processes and best practices, with a demonstrated ability to solve complex problems through collaboration and innovative thinking. The Senior Quality Engineer will be responsible for developing test strategies, authoring automated tests, executing tests, and advising on testability and best practices for quality by participating in all phases of the software development lifecycle. Senior Quality Engineers may directly support development teams as an embedded team member and will implement and enhance the measurement of actionable quality metrics and outcomes related to resilience

title: about IT at RWE
raw_description: Trading IT At RWE, we trade almost terawatt hours of electricity, more than billion cubic meters of gas and around million CO 2 certificates annually. This makes us one of the largest energy traders in Europe - and we owe this primarily to our IT teams. As part of Trading IT, you’ll be the interface between energy trading and the rest of our business. To help us shape the market processes of tomorrow, you’ll get to learn and manage technologies including artificial and business intelligences and data warehouses. about IT at RWE Trading IT At RWE, we trade almost terawatt hours of electricity, more than billion cubic meters of gas and around million CO 2 certificates annually. title: Explore career options in China
raw_description: Our office locations across Asia’s markets Our offices at a glance Beijing, China Shanghai, China Mumbai, India Jakarta, Indonesia Tokyo, Japan Singapore Beijing China In, RWE successfully entered the Chinese market and since then has grown in its activities. Today, the RWE Group is mainly present through its wholly-owned subsidiary RWE Supply & Trading (Shanghai) Co. Ltd., with offices in both Beijing and Shanghai. Explore career options in China Shanghai China RWE Supply & Trading (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. is based in Shanghai - a key hub for energy and trade in Asia. The office focuses on international and domestic energy transactions within China, while exploring opportunities in conventional and renewable energy markets. It also shares expertise in trading and asset optimisation, seeking growth in line with local regulations. Explore career options in China Mumbai India RWE Supply & Trading (India) Private Limited, a wholly owned RWE Supply & Trading subsidiary, opened its office in November. Located in India’s financial capital of Mumbai, RWEST IPL consolidates RWEST’s Indian commodity trading and marketing activities. As part of RWEST’s growth strategy, RWEST IPL focuses on building a commodities business in India. Discover job opportunities in India Jakarta Indonesia Our office in Jakarta is small, but crucial. raw_description: 7 Million jobs from tax credits that will dramatically expand access to solar, wind, and other clean energy sources; Grid modernization: Nearly 40, jobs from loans and grants to make our electricity transmission infrastructure more resilient, reliable, efficient, and accessible; and Rural electric cooperatives: More than 90, jobs from loans for rural electric cooperatives to support clean energy and economic development in rural communities. These investments will support clean air, a livable climate, and the creation of more than, jobs over the 10 years. Efficient Buildings:, Jobs The bill’s investments to make homes and offices more energy efficient, healthier, and more climate-resilient will create more than,000 jobs over the. That includes: Energy efficient buildings tax credits: Nearly, jobs from tax credits to support residential and commercial building retrofits and new home construction that boosts energy efficiency; Home energy rebates: More than, jobs from rebates that will make energy efficiency upgrades more affordable for households so as to cut energy costs and pollution; and Affordable housing: Nearly 10, jobs from investments to retrofit affordable housing units to be more water and energy efficient and climate resilient

raw_description: Wir sind ein globales EnerTech-Unternehmen, das mit innovativen Wärmepumpen-Komplettlösungen die Energiewende vorantreibt. Seitinstallieren wir diese nachhaltige Technologie mit eigenen Fachkräften und setzen damit neue Maßstäbe für die Zukunft der Energieversorgung. Dabei sind unsere Ökostromtarife nur die Basis. Außerdem arbeiten wir aktiv an der flächendeckenden Einführung von Smart Metern, Elektromobilitätslösungen und der eigenen Stromerzeugung aus Wind- und Sonnenenergie. Wir haben kein geringeres Ziel, als die global benötigte Energie % nachhaltig zu gestalten. Global, agil und zukunftsorientiert verändern wir den Energiemarkt mit neuer Technologie zum Besseren. Doch all das gelingt nur mit deinem Engagement! Werde Teil unseres Teams und hilf uns, die Zukunft der Energieversorgung zu gestalten. Gemeinsam schaffen wir eine Energiezukunft, die nicht nur gut für den Planeten, sondern auch für dein Karma ist. 💚 Finde deinen Traumjob bei Octopus Energy und werde Teil der Energierevolution! - Reports to: Head of Communications - Preferred location: Europe or Asia (GMT +/- 7 hours) - Annual Salary: Compensation for this role is benchmarked at EUR 73,000 and adjusted based on the employee’s location and aligned local salary bands. - Working Hours: Full time role at 5
raw_description: Reports to: Head of Communications - Preferred location: Europe or Asia (GMT +/- 7 hours) - Annual Salary: Compensation for this role is benchmarked at EUR 73,000 and adjusted based on the employee’s location and aligned local salary bands. - Working Hours: Full time role at 5 days/week About Climate Catalyst Climate Catalyst is a non-profit dedicated to galvanizing the collective power of business, investors, and civil society to influence and accelerate policy change in Asia and Europe. Our vision is a just, prosperous world in which global warming is limited to 1.5°C. Climate Catalyst works to strengthen collaboration and mobilise new actors on pivotal climate challenges in heavy emitting industries. We build power and compel political leaders to act at the speed and scale needed to tackle the climate crisis. We do this by: - Working behind the scenes with stakeholders in Europe and Asia to identify critical climate issues where action to date has been limited, and the potential for collaboration is high. - Bringing together renowned experts and new entrants to share ideas, and design and deliver creative campaigns that secure decisive changes in national and regional policy to deliver sectoral tipping points and secure significant emissions reductions. - Advising on strategy, offering grants and bringing creative communications, advocacy and business expertise to build power across the climate community and catalyse action. To date, we’ve used this model successfully to mobilise the business community in support of the EU Nature Restoration Law, the first legislation for peatlands protection across Europe, and spark collective action in India that led to a commitment from the Ministry of Steel to develop a green steel roadmap. About the role Communications and narrative building is a core part of Climate Catalyst’s model for impact. This role will be instrumental in delivering impactful and exciting digital communications that support our programmes delivery impact - by informing, influencing and inspiring action. It will also contribute to building Climate Catalyst’s reputation with our key stakeholders across Europe, Asia and globally. You will build our presence and impact by developing digital communication strategies to support our programmes in Europe, India and Indonesia, managing our digital channels and supporting colleagues to grow their online presence. It will support our already established programmes of work on aviation and chemicals in Europe and steel in Asia and support the team with digital expertise as we grow new programmes of work. Climate Catalyst commits to deliver our communications for and through partners. Therefore this role will not only support our internal teams, but with partners across our networks and coalitions to co-build strategies for collective impact. You will also support the Head of Communications in building and protecting the Climate Catalyst brand. What you will do Strategy: - Contribute to Climate Catalyst’s overall communications strategy. - Lead the development of impactful digital strategies to support programme teams to deliver impact across Europe and Asia, including reporting and evaluation. - Advise colleagues on social media best practice, supporting them to grow their professional profiles. - Work with partners and Climate Catalyst coalitions to co-build shared digital strategies and to share and drive digital best practice across the wider ecosystems in which we work. - Manage and monitor Climate Catalyst’s own digital channels - including website and social media - and regularly suggest ways to improve and enhance our digital presence. - Lead the development of thought leadership and reputation building content to help establish Climate Catalyst and its staff as trusted experts in energising collective action on pivotal climate challenges. - Lead and implement SEO strategy, working with the wider team to improve the visibility and performance of our learning hub. Content development: - Lead the content creation for Climate Catalyst’s digital channels, including imagery, graphics, simple reports and briefings and videos, using tools like Canva. - Write and edit copy for Climate Catalyst’s digital channels, including the website, email, newsletters, social media and other digital products. - Lead on scheduling and posting of social media content, and interact with responses. - Keep all templates -date and develop new ones where needed. - Maintain Climate Catalyst’ brand assets, including our image library, icon and graphic library etc. Administration: - Help ensure all digital and communication outputs are on brand, and support teams to meet best practice for brand consistency. - Help ensure Climate Catalyst is meeting best practice standards for digital communications. - Support budget management of digital activities. - Support team with monitoring and reporting requests, and keep our internal dashboard up to day to showcase digital impact. - Support the team to ensure compliance with data protection regulations, and ensure use of tools - i.e. HubSpot, in line with these regulations. - Manage relationships with external consultants and agencies where needed. Innovation and learning: - Keep on top of trends and identify opportunities for experimentation in digital output. - Run internal and partner learning sessions to support broader growth of digital expertise and best practice

title: Head of Corporate Branding
pay_candidate_snippets: As a trusted solar company in Malaysia, we have developed more than 2,000MW of ongoing and completed projects across 8 countries in the Asia Pacific region Recognized as a top-tier solar company, Solarvest continues to drive the growth of solar Malaysia, delivering innovative and
raw_description: Responsible for defining, owning, and governing the Group’s corporate brand strategy to strengthen brand equity, trust, and long-term reputation across all stakeholder touchpoints. This role ensures the company’s brand clearly reflects its clean energy mission, sustainability leadership, and corporate values, while remaining consistent, credible, and compliant in a regulated and public-listed environment. Key Responsibilities 1. Corporate Brand Strategy & Positioning - Develop and lead the Group’s corporate brand strategy, positioning the company as a trusted clean energy leader. - Define and articulate the brand purpose, narrative, values, and visual identity aligned with the company’s long-term strategy and ESG commitments. - Ensure brand positioning supports investor confidence, stakeholder trust, and market differentiation. 2. Brand Governance & Consistency - Own and enforce brand governance frameworks, guidelines, and approval processes across the Group and subsidiaries. - Ensure consistent application of brand identity, tone of voice, and messaging across all corporate, marketing, and internal communications. - Oversee brand usage across digital platforms, corporate materials, events, and external partnerships. 3. Corporate Reputation & ESG Branding - Lead corporate branding initiatives that support reputation management, sustainability storytelling, and ESG communications. - Partner with Sustainability, Corporate Communications, and Investor Relations teams to ensure credible and compliant ESG narratives. - Support thought leadership initiatives to strengthen the Group’s standing in the clean energy and sustainability space. 4. Group & Stakeholder Branding - Lead branding initiatives across the Group, including subsidiaries, joint ventures, and regional operations. - Ensure brand alignment across internal stakeholders, employees, partners, and government bodies. - Partner with Human Resources and cross-functional teams including Sustainability, QSHE, Risk & Compliance, Legal, Investor Relations, Corporate Affairs, Operations, and Finance to strengthen employer branding, ensure brand narratives accurately reflect ESG commitments, governance standards, safety culture, and responsible business practices, and support corporate reputation management in alignment with stakeholder engagement outcomes, materiality priorities, and public-listed company governance requirements. 5. Leadership & Team Management - Build, mentor, and lead the corporate branding team, fostering high standards and brand ownership. - Manage external agencies, consultants, and creative partners to ensure quality and strategic alignment. - Oversee branding budgets, timelines, and performance outcomes. 6. Brand Performance & Evolution - Monitor brand health, perception, and equity through research, insights, and stakeholder feedback. - Lead brand refreshes or evolution initiatives as the company grows, expands, or enters new markets. - Stay abreast of clean energy trends, sustainability narratives, and best practices in corporate branding. Solarvest is Malaysia’s leading, largest, and award-winning integrated solar provider, with over 10 years of expertise and a strong track record. As a trusted solar company in Malaysia, we have developed more than 2,000MW of ongoing and completed projects across 8 countries in the Asia Pacific region Recognized as a top-tier solar company, Solarvest continues to drive the growth of solar Malaysia, delivering innovative and reliable solar energy solutions that power businesses, communities, and a sustainable future

Building on our expertise in energy efficiency, we’re driving the material change needed to renew the promise of energy.
Carbon reduction.
Electrification.

Resource Innovations is seeking an Energy Efficiency Program Delivery Analyst to join our growing team in Massachusetts and New Hampshire. This role will blend relationship-driven outreach with program coordination, process optimization, and hands-on project support to help deliv
raw_description: Resource Innovations is seeking an Energy Efficiency Program Delivery Analyst to join our growing team in Massachusetts and New Hampshire. This role will blend relationship-driven outreach with program coordination, process optimization, and hands-on project support to help deliver high-performing utility energy efficiency programs. In this highly collaborative role, you will act as a key connector between utility clients, vendors, contractors, and internal teams. You will balance front-end engagement with back-end program execution, helping to onboard and support vendors, move projects forward, and continuously improve the processes and systems that drive program success. This role is well suited for someone with approximately 3+ years of experience who is comfortable taking on a range of responsibilities and making an immediate impact. The right candidate enjoys building relationships, solving problems, improving workflows, and keeping things moving in a fast-paced environment. Success in this role will be measured by strong partner relationships, consistent project momentum, program participation, operational efficiency, excellent communication internally and with client, and the ability to identify and implement process improvements that enhance program performance. Resource Innovations (RI) is a women-led energy transformation firm focused on impact. Building on our expertise in energy efficiency, we're constantly expanding our portfolio of clean energy solutions to guide utilities through increasingly complex, connected challenges. Load flexibility. Electrification. Carbon reduction. With every step, we're leading the charge to power change. Duties and Responsibilities - Serve as a primary point of contact for vendors, contractors, utility partners, and internal teams, building strong, trust-based relationships that support program participation and long-term engagement - Deliver vendor onboarding, training, and ongoing engagement, ensuring clear understanding of program requirements, tools, and processes - Drive project advancement through proactive communication, coordination, and follow-up, helping projects move efficiently from opportunity through completion - Support customers and vendors through the application process, helping to remove barriers, resolve issues, and ensure timely submissions - Balance external engagement with internal coordination, ensuring alignment between vendors, program requirements, and internal teams - Report on project and program activity, maintain trackers and databases, and ensure data accuracy and visibility across systems - Develop and support vendor-facing communications, including program updates, outreach emails, and educational materials - Identify process gaps, inefficiencies, or recurring issues, and recommend practical improvements to enhance program operations and scalability - Collaborate cross-functionally with program management, engineering, software, data, and marketing teams to improve workflows, reporting, and program delivery - Adapt quickly to evolving program needs, market conditions, and client priorities while maintaining a high level of responsiveness and accountability - Other duties as assigned. Resource Innovations (RI) is an energy transformation firm. Women-led, purpose built and focused on impact, we’re constantly expanding our portfolio of solutions to guide utilities through increasingly complex, connected challenges. Load flexibility. Electrification. Carbon reduction. Building on our expertise in energy efficiency, we’re driving the material change needed to renew the promise of energy. Discover how at www.resource-innovations.com

raw_description: Resource Innovations is seeking a full-time Program Delivery Consultant to support programs within our Northwest transportation electrification portfolio. This role will directly contribute to advancing utility- and government-funded transportation electrification efforts operating across Oregon and Washington. This is an excellent opportunity for professionals looking to build their career in the clean energy space and gain hands-on experience in clean energy program administration. In this role, you will support an existing project delivery team to meet program goals and objectives, including reviewing transportation electrification grant applications, coordinating program activities with partners and clients, and executing on key deliverables. raw_description: Solarvest Holdings Berhad is a Bursa-listed clean energy company on a mission to accelerate Malaysia's transition to renewable energy. As we grow our brand presence and market position, we are looking for a Head of Marketing who is not just a strategist — but a creative thinker and confident leader who can bring ideas to life, shape compelling narratives, and inspire both internal teams and external audiences. This is a senior leadership role that sits at the intersection of brand strategy, product marketing, and marketing operations. You will define the brand, drive -market strategy, and build a marketing function that reflects the ambition and values of a company that genuinely believes in a cleaner future. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES 1. Corporate Brand Strategy & Governance - Own and define the Group's corporate brand strategy — positioning Solarvest as a credible, inspiring clean energy leader in Malaysia and the region. - Establish brand governance frameworks and ensure consistency across all touchpoints, subsidiaries, campaigns, and external partners. - Lead corporate reputation management and ESG/sustainability narrative, aligned with Solarvest's listed company obligations and stakeholder expectations. 2. Creative Direction & Brand Storytelling - Bring the Solarvest brand to life through bold, distinctive creative ideas — from campaign concepts to content, events, and brand experiences. - Shape and own the brand voice, visual identity, and storytelling approach across all channels and audiences. - Challenge conventional thinking — bring fresh perspectives and industry-leading ideas that set Solarvest apart in a competitive and growing market. 3. Product Marketing & -Market - Lead product marketing across Solarvest's portfolio — developing clear positioning, value propositions, and messaging for each product and service. - Drive -market strategy for new product launches, ensuring sales teams are equipped with the right tools, materials, and messaging to win. - Translate complex clean energy solutions into compelling narratives for B2B & B2C customers, partners, investors, and the public. - Build and maintain strong relationships with media, industry associations, government agencies, and strategic partners to support commercial growth. - Work closely with the sales and business development teams to generate quality leads and support revenue growth. 4. ESG & Sustainability Narrative - Partner with the Sustainability and Investor Relations teams to develop credible and compelling ESG brand communications. - Lead sustainability storytelling initiatives that build trust with stakeholders, investors, and the public. 5. Marketing Operations & Team Leadership - Oversee full marketing operations — budgeting, agency management, vendor negotiations, and performance tracking. - Build, lead, and mentor a high-performing marketing team with a culture of creativity, accountability, and continuous improvement. Have the authority to plan and structure the marketing team as the business evolves. - Act as a strong, visible leader across the business — influencing at C-suite level and driving alignment across departments. 6. AI & Innovation - Lead the adoption of AI across the marketing function — setting the direction, identifying the right tools, and encouraging the team to embrace smarter ways of working. - Stay ahead of emerging AI and marketing technology trends — bringing fresh thinking and relevant best practices into Solarvest's marketing approach. - Champion a culture of innovation — inspiring the team to continuously explore, test, and apply new technologies that improve marketing effectiveness and efficiency. Solarvest is Malaysia’s leading, largest, and award-winning integrated solar provider, with over 10 years of expertise and a strong track record. As a trusted solar company in Malaysia, we have developed more than 2,000MW of ongoing and completed projects across 8 countries in the Asia Pacific region Recognized as a top-tier solar company, Solarvest continues to drive the growth of solar Malaysia, delivering innovative and reliable solar energy solutions that power businesses, communities, and a sustainable future

This person will manage the -end workflow for our research projects, and coordinate the production of research reports. The Herald Group is a full service, bipartisan public affairs firm in Washington, DC, that provides strategic communications counsel to major corporations, trade associations and other organizations seeking to advance public policy positions in federal and state legislative arenas. We provide a fast-paced, energetic work environment, and this position will have daily interaction with senior staff and clients. This is a full time, paid position. raw_description: Who We Are At Murmuration, we believe that America’s promise is shaped and reshaped by the best ideas and ideals of its communities, and the dreams of the people who believe in a better life for themselves, their families, and each other. We help organizations build power in their communities in four key ways: we organize a network of values-aligned partners; we provide deep, data-driven insights into people, places, and perspectives; we develop tools that make organizing and engagement easy and more effective; and we offer services that strengthen our partners’ capacity to lead change in their communities. We envision an America where every community has what it needs to help people lead healthy, free, and dignified lives. We work to redesign the systems and structures we all depend on — how we learn, live, govern, and solve problems — so that they are just, equitable, resilient, and rooted in shared responsibility. By strengthening the ties that hold communities together, we aim for civic life defined by collective action and care, with effective leadership that truly represents everyone. We are a collaborative, curious, and creative team of organizers, scientists, teachers, technologists, campaign veterans, and more who share the unwavering belief that we can use our gifts in service of transforming America — together. We’ve built our team guided by the belief that the whole is greater than the sum of its parts. And so we support each other relentlessly — rallying together to face challenges the same way we celebrate each other’s wins. About the Position Murmuration is seeking a highly organized and experienced Senior Project Manager for its Campaigns team. Murmuration’s Campaigns Team helps partners design and execute smarter strategies from the ground up — powered by the best civic data and tools in the country. We provide high-impact consulting and campaign services to local organizations who are closest to solutions. We combine deep campaign expertise with the most advanced civic data infrastructure and offer a full suite of services to help change-makers build power and meet key moments. The Senior Project Manager will report directly to the Senior Manager of Campaign Engagement, working in close partnership to streamline workflows, manage timelines, and mitigate risks across a diverse portfolio of projects. This person will serve as an integral part of the Campaigns team, providing essential structural support and operational excellence. Crucially, this individual will also act as a critical point of contact and support system for Murmuration's external partners, ensuring transparent communication, aligned expectations, and efficient collaboration throughout the campaign lifecycle. Job Level: P4 What You’ll Do - Serve as the primary project manager for multiple, high-stakes campaigns, ensuring seamless coordination, precise execution, and transparent communication across all internal and external stakeholders; - Design, implement, and rigorously manage detailed campaign plans and timelines. This includes developing clear meeting agendas, capturing comprehensive notes, and executing proactive follow-up to ensure all campaigns and engagements are driven to successful, on-time completion; - Serve as the key operational contact for all external partners, expertly managing inbound inquiries and communication flow; - Provide strategic, trusted support and counsel to a diverse set of stakeholders—from grassroots student and family advocates to C-suite and senior organizational leaders—to maximize campaign effectiveness and partnership health; - Establish and maintain project management tools and templates to drive organizational efficiency and consistency; - Proactively identify, analyze, and mitigate potential risks and roadblocks that could jeopardize campaign timelines or outcomes; - Track, measure, and report on overall project health, presenting regular status updates to leadership; - Facilitate cross-functional retrospectives and post-campaign analysis to capture lessons learned and continuously improve future campaign strategy and execution; and - Manage budget tracking and vendor contracts. title: Senior Manager, Social
pay_candidate_snippets: Simple Mills was also a 2020 Health Magazine Snack Award Winner!
raw_description: Company Description Simple Mills is on a mission to transform the way food is made—creating nourishing products with astounding ingredients and amazing taste. Founded in 2012 by Katlin Smith, our brand has redefined center-aisle grocery by delivering better-for-you products made with unique, nutrient-dense ingredients. Today, we’re a category leader and the #1 natural brand in multiple segments, with distribution in over 30, stores nationwide. Now, as part of Flowers Foods, we’re entering an exciting new phase of growth—expanding our reach, accelerating innovation, and amplifying brand awareness, all while preserving the integrity of what makes Simple Mills special. Our commitment to using simple, high-quality ingredients ensures that every bite is both delicious and nutritious. Whether it’s watermelon seed flour in our cookies or butternut squash in our crackers, we craft our products with innovative, wholesome ingredients that deliver extraordinary taste. Our work has been recognized by Fast Company as a Brand That Matters and a Most Innovative Company, and has been featured in Vogue, Forbes, Women’s Health, and NPR’s How I Built This. In 2024, Inc. named us one of its Best Workplaces, reflecting our dedication to fostering an inspiring and inclusive environment where passionate individuals can make a difference. Join us as we shape the future of food. at www.simplemills.com. Role Description The Senior Manager, Social will play a pivotal role in shaping how Simple Mills shows up in culture, leading the -day strategy and execution of our organic social presence across platforms. This individual will translate brand and business priorities into a clear, differentiated social approach, guiding content direction and partnering closely with agency teams to deliver consistent, high-quality output. As a key member of the Integrated Marketing team, this role operates as a thought partner to leadership, contributing to direction while owning execution, performance, and continuous optimization. The ideal candidate is both creatively instinctive and analytically grounded, with a strong point of view on content, platforms, and what drives engagement. They are deeply attuned to cultural and consumer conversations, and able to translate trends in health, wellness, and sustainability into relevant, social-native storytelling. This role reports to the Senior Director, Integrated Marketing and responsibilities will include: Social Strategy & Channel Leadership - Shape and refine Simple Mills’ organic social strategy across Instagram, TikTok,, and (with Pinterest, YouTube, X, and Threads as secondary platforms). - Translate integrated marketing plans into platform-specific strategies, content plans, and success measures, ensuring clarity in what each channel is driving. - Develop TikTok Shop strategy and execution in partnership with external partners, ensuring alignment to broader social and business goals. - Identify and test opportunities to evolve channel approach, including new formats, tools, and platform capabilities, bringing forward data-informed recommendations. - Partner closely with Brand and Integrated Marketing teams to support launches, retail moments, influencers, and key initiatives. Simple Mills is a female-founded business, started by Katlin Smith in 2012 out of her own Kitchen. Company Description Simple Mills is on a mission to transform the way food is made—creating nourishing products with astounding ingredients and amazing taste. Founded in 2012 by Katlin Smith, our brand has redefined center-aisle grocery by delivering better-for-you products made with unique, nutrient-dense ingredients. Today, we’re a category leader and the #1 natural brand in multiple segments, with distribution in over 30, stores nationwide. Now, as part of Flowers Foods, we’re entering an exciting new phase of growth—expanding our reach, accelerating innovation, and amplifying brand awareness, all while preserving the integrity of what makes Simple Mills special. Our commitment to using simple, high-quality ingredients ensures that every bite is both delicious and nutritious. Whether it’s watermelon seed flour in our cookies or butternut squash in our crackers, we craft our products with innovative, wholesome ingredients that deliver extraordinary taste. Our work has been recognized by Fast Company as a Brand That Matters and a Most Innovative Company, and has been featured in Vogue, Forbes, Women’s Health, and NPR’s How I Built This. In 2024, Inc. named us one of its Best Workplaces, reflecting our dedication to fostering an inspiring and inclusive environment where passionate individuals can make a difference. Join us as we shape the future of food. Learn more at www.simplemills.com.
